data.description:
# P1.4 — rebar gc + maintenance doctrine (bounded growth, recovery-safe)

## Context (problem + research)

Every mutation is a committed file, and the write path sets `gc.auto=0`
(`_store/event_append.py:64 _ensure_gc_auto_zero`) so a background `git gc` can't
prune the reflog commits the reset-recovery net relies on (now in
`rebar._store.sync:54-77`, `reset --hard origin/tickets` for unrelated/diverged
histories, "recoverable via reflog"). The cost is **unbounded** loose-object/pack
growth on long-lived `tickets` branches. git-bug packs ops into fewer objects with
a cache; Fossil's SQLite sidesteps small-object blowup. rebar sells "built to
scale" but ships no safe reclaim path. Akka's distributed-data pruning teaches the
discipline: gate discard on dissemination + a TTL marker, never wall-clock alone.

## Evidence (experiments)

- EXP6: a discarded commit **survives** `git gc --prune=14.days.ago` but is
  **destroyed** by `--prune=now` -> a conservative window is mandatory.
- EXP-R6 (real code): the gc recipe on the **real `.tickets-tracker` orphan-branch
  worktree** packed 26 -> 0 loose objects and `rebar show` still read correctly.

## What / Implementation detail

Operator-only `rebar gc` (**not** over MCP, like `init`), under the write lock
(I5) and only when not mid-recovery (reuse
`rebar._store.lock.check_no_rebase_in_progress` — no leading underscore):
1. `git -C <tracker> reflog expire --expire=<window> --expire-unreachable=<window> --all`
   (BOTH flags — EXP6: unreachable objects are governed by `--expire-unreachable`/
   `--prune`).
2. `git -C <tracker> gc --prune=<window>.ago` (**never `--prune=now`**); conservative
   default `gc.reflog_window` = 14 days that must exceed the worst-case offline/
   un-pushed interval.
3. Report bytes reclaimed. A `--compact-first` flag runs per-ticket `compact`
   over eligible tickets first (I1/I9-safe: SNAPSHOT folds, `*.retired` renames
   union). Document the "compact then gc" cadence in `docs/concurrency.md`.

## Success Criteria

- `rebar gc` reclaims space and reports it; reads work afterward.
- A `gc`-then-recover regression proves `_store.sync` recovery still works after gc
  with the default window (recovery must not need reflog older than the window).
- Refuses to run mid-recovery; absent from the MCP surface.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] operator-only `rebar gc` (reflog expire --expire+--expire-unreachable, gc --prune=<window>.ago, never now)
- [ ] `--compact-first` runs per-ticket compaction before packing
- [ ] mid-recovery guard via `check_no_rebase_in_progress`; not exposed over MCP
- [ ] gc-then-recover regression test + bytes-reclaimed sanity on a synthetic many-event ticket
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` documents the compact-then-gc cadence and window rationale

See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.4).
data.title: P1.4 — rebar gc + maintenance doctrine (bounded growth, recovery-safe)
